beats.narration_text: Bula — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: songbird, the prompt-sequencing director. It owns the one law every generated-clip chain in the toolkit must follow: Entry, Beat, Exit. Every clip points at the next.

props.runningText: Entry → Beat → Exit → Entry → Beat → Exit...
output: THE SEQUENCING LAW: Entry–Beat–Exit per clip
THE PARAMETER RULE: style string appends verbatim
Session state: new song = full reset
Plug engine: one cliffhanger, no resolution

beats.narration_text: The skill has two laws and one session contract. The sequencing law governs every prompt chain: Entry inherits from what came before, Beat delivers one clear idea, Exit points at what comes next. The parameter rule governs style strings: one style string appends to every prompt, unchanged. The session contract governs context: song material, reset boundaries, remix scope. Six engines apply these laws to specific tasks — song, boogie, plug, one-sixty-nine, colorful, and the seasonal Xmas preset.

props.title: Two laws. One session contract. Six engines.
files: SKILL.md — THE SEQUENCING LAW + THE PARAMETER RULE + engines
skills/make/music-video/ — song engine: per-beat prompt sequencing
skills/make/dance-video/ — boogie engine: dancer continuity prompts
props.calloutSub: the law every prompt chain follows

beats.narration_text: Songbird is a director, not a builder — it authors the prompt chain, then the builder skill generates the clips. The flow: name the material and the engine; apply the sequencing law per clip — Entry from the prior clip's Exit, one Beat, Exit to the next Entry; append the style string to every prompt verbatim; pass the chain to Higgsfield or Hailuo for generation. Plug and one-sixty-nine are one-shot outputs — no chain, just the output rule.

props.title: Material → sequence → style-string → generate.
props.inputLabel: song / lyrics / still / piece (depends on engine)
props.outputLabel: prompt chain (generation is the builder skill's job)

beats.narration_text: The plug: every skill in this series was written by the same system it describes — except the one that doesn't exist yet. Watch the full story below. That is the plug. One sentence. It raises the question of what the missing skill is without naming it. The CTA follows verbatim. Noir. No summary. No resolution. The rule is: tease the conflict, never provide it.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: the sequencing law. Every generated-clip chain has three parts per clip: Entry, which inherits motion or context from what came before; Beat, which delivers one clear readable event; and Exit, which points at what comes next. Without the Exit, each clip is complete in itself — and a chain of ten complete moments is not a sequence, it is a grab-bag. The continuity lanes — visual space, lighting, character, camera path, and musical key, tempo, groove — carry coherence across the whole chain.

props.heading: Entry → Beat → Exit. Every clip, every chain.
props.body: The Sequencing Law applies to every generated-clip prompt chain in the toolkit: story-film, dance-video, music-video, lyric-resync, kids-video. Each clip must (1) inherit motion/context from the prior clip's Exit, (2) deliver one clear readable mini-event, (3) point at the next Entry. Continuity lanes — visual (space, lighting, character, camera) and musical (key, tempo, groove) — hold across the entire chain. Without them, ten clips are ten disconnected moments.
props.quote: Never disconnected moments — every chain is one continuous experience.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: the parameter rule. When a prompt chain carries a style string — model parameters, aspect flags, look descriptors — that string appends to every prompt line exactly as written. Not paraphrased. Not dropped after the first three prompts because they looked right. Not shortened mid-chain. The rule is mechanical: one string, every line, unchanged. Without it, clips three through ten diverge from clips one and two in ways that can't be corrected in post.

props.heading: One style string. Every prompt. Verbatim.
props.body: THE PARAMETER RULE: when a prompt chain carries a style string (--p …, aspect flags, model params), it appends to EVERY prompt line EXACTLY as written — never altered, never dropped mid-chain. This is how a chain of ten AI-generated clips stays one look end to end. A style string that disappears after clip 3 means clips 4–10 draw from a different visual pool. The rule is mechanical, not creative.
props.quote: appends to EVERY prompt line EXACTLY as written — never altered, never dropped mid-chain

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: session-state logic. A session tracks one song's material — its lyrics, its style, its character states. Remix keeps continuity. Style-swap changes only the style. New song resets everything. The critical rule is the reset boundary: a session that mixes prompts from two songs without a reset produces clips that inherit the wrong character, the wrong key, the wrong groove. A new song is not a variation — it is a new context, and context leaking across sessions produces incoherence that no amount of editing can fix.

props.heading: Session state: reset is the boundary.
props.body: Session-state logic: lyrics/song name = core material. 'style:' swaps look only. 'remix' = variation keeping continuity lanes. 'new song' = full reset of all context. The reset is what keeps two sessions from bleeding into each other. A song's character state, key, groove, and camera path are session-local — carry them across a reset and prompts inherit the wrong world. Reset is not a preference; it is the session's integrity mechanism.
props.quote: A session never mixes material from two songs without an explicit reset.

beats.narration_text: Songbird: the sequencing law, the parameter rule, and a clean session contract. Every clip inherits and exits. Every style string propagates verbatim. Every new song resets the context.

artifactLines: THE SEQUENCING LAW: Entry–Beat–Exit per clip, continuity lanes held
THE PARAMETER RULE: style string verbatim on every prompt line
session contract: remix keeps context; new song resets all
plug engine: one cliffhanger sentence, no resolution, CTA verbatim

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run songbird on a music or dance project. Before you start: does every prompt in your chain carry an Exit that points at the next Entry — not just a complete image, but a motion or context that leads somewhere? And does your style string appear verbatim on every line, or did it silently disappear after prompt three?
